_comment: Per-tool MCP enrichments. Applied on top of the OpenAPI-derived schema at registration time. Supported keys: required (string[]), description_prefix, description_suffix, destructive (bool), example_request, examples ([]), example_names ([string]), max_examples (int).
_intent: Only content that should NOT appear in public REST API docs belongs here: (1) MCP-parser-specific limitations, (2) MCP tool-name cross-references that are meaningless to SDK/curl users, (3) temporary bridges while upstream backend PRs are in flight. Everything else lives in the backend @extend_schema or help_text. See UPSTREAMING.md for migration tracking.

aiagents_upload_knowledge_base_create.description_suffix: KNOWN LIMITATION: this MCP tool cannot accept file bodies (MCP uses JSON-only transport). For file uploads use the Cekura dashboard or the Python SDK (`cekura.agents.upload_knowledge_base(id=..., files=[...])`). This tool currently returns existing KB page metadata only.

scenarios_run_scenarios_create.description_suffix:
The run mode (voice / text / WebRTC) is determined by WHICH run endpoint you call — not by the agent config. The same agent can be run as voice via this endpoint or as text via scenarios_run_scenarios_text_create.

USE WHEN: real phone/SIP call. DO NOT USE for CI or rapid iteration — incurs telephony cost.

scenarios_run_scenarios_text_create.description_suffix:
The run mode is determined by WHICH run endpoint you call — not by the agent config. Calling this endpoint makes the run text-mode regardless of how the agent was created.

USE WHEN: cheap text/chat simulation. DO NOT USE for voice-specific metrics (latency, pitch) — they will be zero. Note: passing `websocket_url` reclassifies the result's `run_type` to `WebSocket`.

scenarios_run_scenarios_pipecat_create.description_suffix:
The run mode is Pipecat/Daily WebRTC — determined by WHICH run endpoint you call, not the agent config.

USE WHEN: agent is deployed on Pipecat/Daily.co. Needs a Pipecat API key on the project.

scenarios_run_scenarios_with_websockets_.description_suffix:
The run mode is custom WebSocket — determined by WHICH run endpoint you call, not the agent config.

USE WHEN: agent exposes its own WebSocket protocol. Each evaluator must carry a `websocket_url`. DO NOT USE for generic text testing — use scenarios_run_scenarios_text_create instead.

metrics_create.description_suffix:
**`add_to_new_agents` default**: when omitted or `true`, this metric is automatically assigned to every new agent created in the project going forward. Set `add_to_new_agents: false` if you want the metric scoped to specific agents only.

**Attaching to evaluators**: after creation, use `scenarios_partial_update` with `metrics: [id1, id2, ...]` to attach metrics to evaluators. The metric IDs must belong to the same agent or project — use `metrics_list` with `agent_id=<id>` to list available metrics for an agent.

metrics_list.description_suffix: **Filter tip**: always pass `agent_id` or `project_id` to scope results — without a filter you get every metric across your account. Example: `metrics_list(agent_id=1234)` returns only metrics accessible to that agent (both agent-level and project-level).

metrics_bulk_create_create.description_suffix: **Input shape**: pass the array of metric objects directly as the `items` parameter. Each item has the same fields as `metrics_create` (name, description, type, eval_type, prompt, project, etc.). Example: `items: [{"name": "Empathy", "type": "llm_judge", "eval_type": "binary_qualitative", "prompt": "...", "project": 1234}, ...]`
